feed.title: Modern Tech & Affiliate Blog
feed.subtitle: High readability tech reviews and affiliate recommendations.

entry.title: How I Automated Chrome Extension Publishing with GitHub Actions

link.title: How I Automated Chrome Extension Publishing with GitHub Actions

entry.content:
Every time I shipped a new version of my Chrome extension, I dreaded the same ritual — bump the version, zip the build folder, log into the Chrome Web Store Developer Dashboard, upload the file, fill in the release notes, and hit publish. It sounds simple, but do it a dozen times and you start wondering: why am I doing this manually?

That’s when I wired up GitHub Actions to handle it for me. Now every merge to main ships my extension automatically. Here’s exactly how I did it.

What You’ll Need Before You Start

Before writing a single line of YAML, make sure you have three things ready: a Google account with Chrome Web Store developer access, an extension already listed on the store (even in draft), and a GitHub repository with your extension code.

You’ll also need to create API credentials from Google. Head to the Google Cloud Console, create a project, enable the Chrome Web Store API, and generate OAuth 2.0 credentials (Client ID + Client Secret). Then use the OAuth playground to get a refresh token scoped to https://www.googleapis.com/auth/chromewebstore. This one-time setup unlocks programmatic publishing.

Storing Secrets in GitHub

Never hardcode credentials. Instead, go to your GitHub repo → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions and add these three secrets:

  • EXTENSION_ID - the unique ID from your Chrome Web Store listing URL
  • CLIENT_ID - from your Google Cloud OAuth credentials
  • CLIENT_SECRET - same place
  • REFRESH_TOKEN - obtained from the OAuth Playground

These values will be injected securely into your workflow at runtime.

The GitHub Actions Workflow

Create a file at .github/workflows/publish.yml in your repo:

name: Publish Chrome Extension

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build extension
        run: npm run build

      - name: Zip the build
        run: zip -r extension.zip dist/

      - name: Upload & Publish to Chrome Web Store
        uses: mnao305/chrome-extension-upload@v5.0.0
        with:
          file-path: extension.zip
          extension-id: ${{ secrets.EXTENSION_ID }}
          client-id: ${{ secrets.CLIENT_ID }}
          client-secret: ${{ secrets.CLIENT_SECRET }}
          refresh-token: ${{ secrets.REFRESH_TOKEN }}
          publish: true

This workflow triggers on every push to main, builds your extension, zips the output, and publishes it to the Chrome Web Store — all without you lifting a finger.

A Few Things Worth Knowing

The publish: true flag submits the extension for review immediately. If you’d rather upload it and review before going live, set publish: false — the new version will sit in draft on the dashboard until you manually publish.

Also make sure your manifest.json version is bumped before merging. The Chrome Web Store rejects uploads with a version number that already exists. A clean approach is to automate version bumping using npm version patch as part of your release process or a separate workflow.

Why This Matters

Beyond saving time, this workflow makes your release process consistent and auditable. Every deployment is tied to a commit, logged in GitHub, and repeatable. If something goes wrong, you can trace it back instantly.

It also removes the human error factor — no more forgetting to update the store listing or uploading the wrong build. The pipeline either passes or it fails loudly, which is exactly what you want in a release process.

Wrapping Up

Setting this up takes about an hour the first time, mostly spent navigating Google’s OAuth flow. But once it’s in place, shipping your Chrome extension becomes as simple as merging a pull request. That’s the kind of automation that quietly makes your development life better every single week.

If you’re building Chrome extensions and still deploying manually, this is the one workflow worth setting up today.
entry.summary: Tired of manually zipping and uploading your Chrome extension updates? Learn how I built a seamless GitHub Actions workflow to automate my deployments in under 10 minutes.

entry.title: Why Moosend Is the Best Email Marketing Platform for Small Businesses and Growing Brands

link.title: Why Moosend Is the Best Email Marketing Platform for Small Businesses and Growing Brands

entry.content:
Email marketing often starts with good intentions. Businesses want to stay connected with customers, promote offers, and build long-term relationships. But after signing up for a platform, many realize the process can become expensive and complicated. Between confusing dashboards, limited automation, and rising subscription costs, email marketing sometimes feels harder than it should be.

That’s where [Moosend stands out. It offers a balance between simplicity and functionality, making it easier for businesses to create campaigns, automate communication, and manage subscribers without a steep learning curve. For startups, freelancers, and growing businesses, having a tool that feels easy from the beginning can make a noticeable difference.

[Image Placeholder: Email marketing dashboard overview]

One of the biggest reasons businesses explore alternatives is cost. Many email marketing tools become expensive as contact lists grow or advanced features are needed. Moosend provides automation, segmentation, and analytics without making users feel locked into high monthly pricing. This makes it appealing for businesses that want strong features while keeping marketing costs under control.

The platform is designed to help users create emails quickly. Its drag-and-drop editor allows professional-looking campaigns to be built without coding knowledge. Templates simplify the process further, helping businesses launch campaigns faster while still maintaining their branding.

[Image Placeholder: Drag and drop email builder]

Automation is another area where Moosend performs well. Instead of manually sending emails, businesses can create workflows based on user behavior. Welcome emails, follow-ups, and abandoned cart reminders can all run automatically. This saves time while creating a more personalized experience for subscribers.

Audience targeting also plays a major role in improving results. Sending the same message to everyone rarely works. Moosend allows businesses to segment contacts based on engagement, preferences, or actions. This helps campaigns feel more relevant, which often leads to better open rates and conversions.

[Image Placeholder: Automation workflow example]

Another advantage is the ability to try the platform without immediate commitment. A free trial with no credit card requirement gives users the chance to explore features before investing. For many businesses, this removes hesitation and makes switching platforms feel less risky.

Email marketing should not feel complicated or expensive. It should help businesses communicate more effectively and save time through automation. For companies looking for an affordable and user-friendly solution, Moosend offers a practical way to manage email campaigns without unnecessary complexity.

[Image Placeholder: Free trial signup screen]

Try Moosend for free and explore the platform here →

Disclaimer: This post contains affiliate links. If you purchase through these links, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.
entry.summary: A comprehensive look at why Moosend is the perfect blend of simplicity and advanced automation for growing businesses.

entry.title: Remote Work Essentials in 2026
entry.content:
The landscape of remote work has stabilized, but the tools we use continue to evolve.

High-Quality Audio is Mandatory

A good microphone and noise-cancellation software are no longer optional. They are the professional equivalent of dressing well for the office.

Asynchronous Communication

Fewer meetings, more structured documentation. Mastering async communication is the highest leverage skill a remote worker can learn.
entry.summary: The tools and habits that define successful remote work today.

entry.title: The Shift to Serverless Architectures
entry.content:
Serverless computing has evolved rapidly. It’s no longer just for simple functions, but for entire enterprise architectures.

The Scalability Advantage

You only pay for what you use, and it scales infinitely. But managing cold starts and state is still a challenge for many teams.
entry.summary: Understanding the benefits and tradeoffs of serverless computing in 2026.

entry.title: Minimalism in Web Design
entry.content:
Minimalist web design isn’t just about white space; it’s about removing friction.

Focus on Content

By stripping away unnecessary visual elements, you direct the user’s focus precisely where it needs to be: the content.
entry.summary: Why less is more when it comes to modern web interfaces.

entry.title: The Future of AI in Software Engineering

link.title: The Future of AI in Software Engineering

entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence is no longer just a buzzword; it is an active participant in the coding process. From auto-completing complex functions to suggesting architectural improvements, AI is fundamentally changing the landscape.

AI as a Pair Programmer

Tools like Copilot and advanced language models act as an always-on pair programmer, reducing the time spent on boilerplate code.

The Evolution of Debugging

Debugging is becoming faster. AI can pinpoint logical errors and suggest fixes before the code even runs in a test environment.
entry.summary: How AI assistants are reshaping the daily workflow of software engineers.

entry.title: Mastering React Hooks in 2026
entry.content:
React Hooks have completely transformed how we write components. In this post, we’ll explore some advanced patterns for creating reusable, highly-performant custom hooks.

Why Custom Hooks?

Custom hooks allow you to extract component logic into reusable functions. This not only cleans up your components but also makes your logic highly testable.

The useFetch Pattern

One of the most common requirements is data fetching. By building a robust useFetch hook, you handle loading states, error boundaries, and caching in one place.

Stay tuned for more React tips!
entry.summary: A deep dive into custom React Hooks for modern web development.

entry.title: Top 3 Coding Laptops for 2026: The Ultimate Comparison

link.title: Top 3 Coding Laptops for 2026: The Ultimate Comparison

entry.content:
Choosing the right machine for software development is one of the most critical decisions for any professional. A slow laptop doesn’t just waste seconds—it breaks your focus.

In this deep dive, we’ve benchmarked the latest releases to bring you the definitive list of the best developer laptops of the year.

Quick Comparison Table

If you’re in a hurry, here’s the breakdown of our top 3 picks across different categories.

Product Features Best for Price / Link
MacBook Pro (M4 Max)
M4 Max Chip, 64GB Unified RAM, 1TB SSD Mobile App Development & ML Check Price
Framework Laptop 13
Modular parts, 13th Gen Intel, 100% repairable Open Source Developers Check Price
Dell XPS 16
RTX 4060, 4K OLED, Intel Core Ultra 7 Windows/WSL Productivity Check Price

1. The Powerhouse: MacBook Pro (M4 Max)

The Apple transition to silicon continues to dominate. For high-performance developers working with massive React Native projects or training local ML models, the M4 Max is virtually untouchable.

Key Pros:

  • Incredible battery life (up to 20 hours)
  • Liquid Retina XDR display is best-in-class
  • Quiet cooling even under heavy load

2. The Open Source Champion: Framework Laptop 13

If you value the right to repair and modularity, the Framework Laptop is the undisputed winner. It allows you to swap components as your needs evolve, making it a future-proof investment.

Key Pros:

  • Fully modular design
  • Excellent Linux compatibility
  • Respects user privacy and control

3. The Performance King: Dell XPS 16

For those who prefer Windows/WSL2, the XPS 16 remains the gold standard. Its design is futuristic, and the raw power from NVIDIA’s latest RTX series is impressive.

Conclusion: Which one should you buy?

If you’re in the Apple ecosystem, the MacBook Pro is the obvious choice. However, if portability and customizability are your priorities, the Framework Laptop 13 is a refreshing alternative that actually works as advertised.

Disclaimer: We may receive small commissions when you click on links and make purchases.
entry.summary: Expert guide on optimizing your workspace for remote efficiency and mental focus.

entry.title: The Ultimate Guide to Modern Workspace Efficiency

link.title: The Ultimate Guide to Modern Workspace Efficiency

entry.content:
In the age of digital transformation, your physical workspace is more than just a place to sit. It’s an extension of your creative mind.

Why Workspace Design Matters

Research shows that a well-organized environment can increase productivity by up to 20%. Whether you’re a software engineer, a creative designer, or a digital marketer, the logic remains the same: Reduce friction, increase flow.

Product Comparisons: My Top Recommendations

To help you get started, here are my top picks for workspace essentials this year. Each of these has been tested for durability and ergonomic efficiency.

Product Features Best for Price / Link
Ergo Pro Chair
Lumbar support, 4D armrests, breathable mesh Long-duration coding Check Price
UltraWide 34”
144Hz Refresh, 1440p resolution, built-in hub Video editing & multitasking Check Price
Keychron K2
Wireless, Mechanical (Brown switches), Mac/Win Mobile productivity Check Price

The Core Philosophy: Modular & Minimalist

When designing my personal office, I follow three rules:

  1. Everything has a place. If a tool doesn’t have a designated spot, it’s clutter.
  2. Invest in quality over quantity. One premium monitor is better than three budget ones.
  3. Ergonomics is non-negotiable. Your future self will thank you for that high-end chair.

“The secret to successful remote work isn’t just about the tools you use, but how you design the environment around them.”

Conclusion

Building the perfect workspace is an iterative process. Start with the essentials, listen to your body’s feedback, and slowly refine your setup until it feels invisible—a seamless gateway to your best work.

Disclaimer: This post contains affiliate links. If you purchase through these links, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.
entry.summary: Expert guide on optimizing your workspace for remote efficiency and mental focus.
